text: We use a convolutional neural network for text classification, achieving state-of-the-art results on sentiment analysis benchmarks.
The attention mechanism is a key component of the transformer architecture, enabling the model to focus on relevant parts of the input.
Our experiments show that data augmentation leads to significant improvements in model accuracy on small datasets.
We compare BERT with GPT-2 on a range of natural language understanding tasks to evaluate their relative strengths.
The embedding layer converts discrete tokens into continuous vector representations used by subsequent layers of the network.
This paper addresses the problem of machine translation using sequence-to-sequence models with attention.
The learning rate is a critical hyperparameter of the gradient descent optimizer that controls convergence speed.
Our approach produces more coherent summaries compared to previous extractive summarization methods.
The encoder module processes the input sequence and generates hidden representations for the decoder to attend over.
We evaluate our named entity recognition system against the CoNLL-2003 benchmark, achieving an F1 score of 93.2.
